text.content:
Wonderbedlam

A Scenario inspired by Alice's Adventures in Wonderland (Lewis Carroll)

Safety Keywords: Mental illness / Imprisonment / Abuse / Cannibalism

Once upon a time...

The Hunters have been ordered by the Royal Court of England to conduct a discreet investigation into Wonder, an asylum for the treatment of mental illnesses. The asylum is located in York and is managed by the renowned Doctor Alice Liddell, who has patented a cure for insanity that she refuses to disclose to other doctors. Everything is connected to a daring complaint lodged by the Duke of Norfolk, who claims that his daughter Alissa has been locked up inside Wonder, even though the asylum has confirmed that no one by that name is a patient there. The Duke, however, believes that his daughter was voluntarily committed after suffering a serious post-partum depression. Although the girl's son died shortly after birth, her desire to remain at the bedlam appears to be the result of a terrible spell.

The Duke, however, does not tell the Court that he suspects Alissa has been imprisoned because of Alicia, the girl's former employee and lover. Alicia has already infiltrated the asylum by pretending to be insane, and the Duke fears for his daughter's life. In fact, Alissa is kept prisoner and sedated in the Hall of the Mind, waiting for the Director, Alice Liddell, to erase her memories forever and turn her into a new identity. For Alice, the best cure is to let go of everything that troubles us in order to be reborn without burdens.

The story that led...

Alice Liddell has suffered from mental illness throughout her life but, unlike others, was capable of understanding her issues and containing them. She was always fascinated by her own condition, and grew increasingly curious about the mysterious workings of the mind. Despite having no background in the medical field, she worked hard to graduate and after becoming a psychiatrist, she undertook her life's work: the creation of Wonder, a bedlam where the inmates themselves could create their own path to recovery. Alice often encountered so-called treatments that only destroyed patients, ruining them and reducing them to little more than zombies. At the same time, she had begun to study magic in secret and thought that with the right ritual, she could have turned Wonder into a sentient entity, a bedlam where the environment itself was able to assist in the healing process.

Wishing to build a building that could truly help those affected by mental illness, she turned to Hatta Carter, a renowned inventor and a madman himself. Hatta became fascinated with the project and together the two created Wonder. The bedlam would not only be a treatment facility but would also become a place where the environment itself could mold itself around the Patient, acting on an individual basis according to their problems.

After Wonder was finished, however, Alice betrayed Hatta, locking him up in the Basement. Alice had learned to deal with her madness but was afraid that Hatta, who had not come to terms with his, would ruin the marvel she had created. Alice discovered that she could control Wonder with her will alone, bending the place to her wishes. Her own form of insanity had given her a warped perception of the world and through this magic place, she was able to grant it a concrete form. With Wonder, she could enter into the minds of her patients and replace their trauma with new memories. More than a few patients have been permanently healed by having all of their problems erased from existence.

pages.name: The Story at the Arrival of the Hunters

text.content:
The story at the arrival of the Hunters...

The Hunters will quickly discover that Wonder defies all logic, with rooms appearing and disappearing at random, corridors that do not lead to any discernible area of the bedlam, and a powerful arcane force permeating everything within the building itself. Alice will be curious about the Hunters, but she will not voluntarily let them meet Alissa. The Director will be willing to release the girl only if the Hunters prove themselves worthy of learning Wonder's mysteries. At the same time, Alicia will do everything she can to thwart Alice's therapy, hoping to wake her beloved before Alice destroys Alissa's mind for good.

Wonder is divided into various areas: the Entrance Hall with three magic doors leading to the Cure Rooms (the Rabbit Hole, the Labyrinth of Doors, and the Caterpillars' Greenhouse), the Basement accessible through a painted door on the floor, and the Hall of the Mind hidden beyond a mirrored wall. Cheshire, Alice's trained lynx, serves as her eyes and ears throughout the facility. White Rabbits carry cryptic messages that provide clues to navigate Wonder's impossible architecture. The building itself is a living entity powered by madness, teleporting those who try to alter its structure and inflicting random transformations.

The Hunters must navigate this surreal maze, decipher the clues, find both Alicia and Hatta Carter, and confront Alice Liddell before Alissa's identity is permanently erased. But the deeper they go into Wonder, the more they risk losing themselves to its reality-bending madness.

Main NPCs

Alice Liddell - The Director

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am the director of Wonder and, thanks to the power of madness, I can enter people's minds to heal them.

Agenda: Safeguard what I have built within Wonder and all the incredible discoveries that the magic of madness can give to mankind / Solve every problem by transforming it into a Patient to be treated.

Gift - Alice from Other Worlds: As an action, Alice can attempt to possess the body of a person or animal by looking into their eyes. Hunters can make a Defense Check to prevent this. Alice can both act through the body she controls and continue to use her Gifts and Descriptors. The possession ends when Alice's body suffers 1 Wound. Alice cannot control more than 3 common subjects or a single Hunter at the same time.

Alicia - The Young Girl in Love

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a young rebel from the slums who knows how to get by on her own. Thanks to my tenacity, I can overcome every obstacle.

Agenda: I want to save Alissa from this cursed place and run away with her to live out our love in peace.

Alissa of Norfolk

OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I have been physically tested by my stay in Wonder, but my mind is still strong. My love for Alicia kept me in the real world.

Agenda: Escape from Wonder and my old life and make a future with Alicia.

Hatta Carter - The Mad Inventor

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a skilled inventor, the most brilliant of my time. My emotional fragility leads me to frequent mood swings and aggressiveness.

Agenda: I want the company of witty people and not cold automata. I long for the chats I had with Alice.

Gift - Time Clocks: Hatta can manipulate one of his clocks to influence time. 1. Slow: Once per Scene, Hatta increases his OL by 2 when defending against a threat. 2. Speed Up: Once per Scene, Hatta and one of his Automata can act, immediately stopping anyone's action regardless of the situation in the narrative. If an Automaton has already acted, it can act again thanks to this Gift.

Threats and Creatures

Cheshire - The Faithful Lynx

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am an aggressive lynx trained to roam Wonder. My body phases in and out of reality constantly.

Agenda: Serve Alice and keep intruders away from sensitive areas.

Gift - Elusive: Cheshire can disappear and reappear a short distance away. The lynx can also move through doors and walls with impunity, and always move without anyone being able to stop it. Any attack made against Cheshire receives 1 Penalty due to its constant phasing. Cheshire is also immune to the Gift Place of Pure Madness.

The White Rabbit

OL Medium 5 – Threat

Descriptor: A little white rabbit with a message holder around its neck. It fears strangers and tries to escape when spotted.

Gift - Messenger: If captured, the White Rabbit will reveal a message. Roll 1d6: 1-2 "It is not always convenient to knock with the hand"; 3-4 "Knocking is the bare minimum for a respectable person"; 5-6 "When you arrive at a door, it is polite to ask if anyone is home".

The Patients of Wonder

OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: We are mad and desperate, driven to Wonder in the hope of curing our ailments. Madness guides our actions and we no longer understand that there is a world outside the bedlam.

Gift - Why I Am Here: Roll a die to see what problem led them to Wonder:

  1. Violence: The Patient struggles to contain their anger. When they act violently increase their OL by 1.
  2. Kleptomania: The Patient yearns for any object they deem interesting and will do everything to have it. Once per Scene, they can act 2 times consecutively, though the additional Action must be used to attempt to escape and hide with the stolen goods.
  3. Abandoned: The Patient was interned at Wonder to be forgotten. Hardened by loneliness, they gain 1 extra Wound.
  4. Split personality: The Patient changes personality in the face of a specific event, such as a threat. When the new personality acts, its OL becomes Hard 7.
  5. Dementia: The Patient is not fully aware of what is happening around them. They are immune to all attempts at intimidation. Increase their OL by 1 when defending against a violent action.
  6. Illness: The Patient is ill or horribly disfigured. Each action against them receives 1 Penalty.

Caterpillar of the Wonderful Lands

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (2 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a huge carnivorous caterpillar over two meters long. I feed on fresh meat and then lay my eggs in the carcasses of creatures I've devoured.

Gift - Hunter Caterpillar: When a Caterpillar moves, any human-sized subject in its path risks being overwhelmed. Whoever is in the Caterpillar's way must perform a Defense Check in order not to end up knocked down. The Caterpillar inflicts 1 Additional Wound against people prone on the ground.

Rabbit Automaton

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (2 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am an automaton in the service of Hatta Carter. Thanks to my strong legs I can make incredible jumps and crush everything when landing.

Gift - Leap: When it acts, the Automaton can free itself from any engagement with ease by jumping away. If it lands on a victim, it increases its OL by 1 for the attack and if it inflicts 1 Wound, the target will also be knocked to the ground.

Lizard Automaton

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am an automaton in the service of Hatta Carter. Thanks to my large tail I can perform several actions at the same time.

Gift - Tail: The Automaton always acts twice, once with its front legs and once with its long tail, but cannot focus both its actions on the same target.

Key Locations

Entrance Hall

Descriptor: A large hall with a mirror covering an entire wall. Three doors of different sizes (normal, double-sized, half-sized) lead to the Cure Rooms. A tree fountain with fish stands in the center, and a door is painted on the floor. The inscription reads: "In this place, madness becomes reason."

Magic Elements:

  • Gift - Beyond the Mirror: Saying the formula "Beyond madness, beyond the basement, beyond the mirror" reveals a hidden passage to the Hall of the Mind. Hatta Carter knows the complete formula.
  • Gift - The Door to The Below: Tap the painted floor door and say "Is anyone under the house?" to access the Basement.
  • Gift - The Doors to Elsewhere: The three wall doors lead through confusing corridors to the Cure Rooms.

Information: Upon arrival, Hunters wait 30 minutes. A white rabbit delivers a note from the Director: "You are not welcome at Wonder." The exterior has painted fake windows and doors—only the main entrance is real. Magic detection reveals powerful arcane force throughout.

Room 1 - The Rabbit Hole

Descriptor: A series of burrows and tunnels full of dirt and vegetation. In the small rooms and crevices, naked and emaciated patients move like animals, always looking for something to eat.

Random Encounters (roll 1d6):

  1. 1-2: A group of 1d6 + 3 Patients will approach the Hunters in search of food.
  2. 3-4: Alicia is busy fending off a group of aggressive Patients.
  3. 5-6: A White Rabbit passes a few meters from the Hunters.

Room 2 - The Labyrinth of Doors

Descriptor: An infinite series of rooms, all with doors leading into a long corridor. Each room is decorated in a different and macabre way, recreating situations of torture and stress.

Random Encounters (roll 1d6):

  1. 1-2: A group of 1d6 + 3 Patients will approach the Hunters to push them into a room shouting, "We must see what's beyond!"
  2. 3-4: Alicia is busy fending off a group of aggressive Patients.
  3. 5-6: A White Rabbit passes a few meters from the Hunters.

Room 3 - The Caterpillars' Greenhouse

Descriptor: A huge greenhouse full of alien and unknown plants. A strong smell of pollen saturates the air, making it difficult to breathe. A dense yellow fog sharply reduces visibility.

Random Encounters (roll 1d6):

  1. 1-2: Two Patients are escaping from a Caterpillar of the Wonderful Lands.
  2. 3-4: Alicia is busy fending off 2 Caterpillars of the Wonderful Lands.
  3. 5-6: A White Rabbit passes a few meters from the Hunters.

pages.name: Locations - Basement and Hall of Mind

text.content:
The Basement

Descriptor: A huge underground hall filled with food and comfortable cushions to sit on. Unnatural darkness surrounds the room, making it difficult to distinguish the walls.

Information: The Basement is where Patients arrive after figuring out how to open the floor door in the Entrance Hall. In Alice's theory, reaching the Basement means they have understood how to leave behind their issues and psychoses represented in the Cure Rooms. Here, Patients can refresh themselves and rest before being picked up in their sleep and taken to the Hall of the Mind.

Prisoner: Hatta Carter is imprisoned in the Basement, kept alive by Alice to be used for future bedlam expansion projects. Hatta has gone mad with loneliness and built two Automata (Rabbit and Lizard) as pretend tea companions. The tablecloth on his tea table is actually the blueprint of Wonder, revealing the formula for going beyond the mirror. Hatta will strongly object if Hunters try to take the map, as it would ruin his table setting.

Key Information from Hatta:

  • Those who pass through the Cure Rooms cycle can access the Basement to refresh and wait for transfer to the Hall of the Mind
  • The Director keeps Hatta here for future expansions
  • Hatta is a prisoner but built Wonder and knows the formula for going beyond the mirror
  • If Hunters point out he can escape with his knowledge, he will reveal the formula in amazement, then return to his madness
  • Hatta doesn't know the Director's real name but knows she's a renowned doctor and "not really a bad person" - just doing "indelicate acts in the name of scientific progress"

Warning: When Hunters attempt to leave, Hatta will beg them to stay, becoming aggressive and unleashing his Automata to keep his new guests.

The Hall of the Mind

Descriptor: A series of rooms painted entirely in white. The doors themselves do not have handles and it takes a careful look to distinguish them from the walls. A large central hall gathers all the Patients, lying on rows of beds, asleep in a state of trance. The distant ticking of a clock breaks an otherwise unnatural silence, creating a hypnotic echo.

Information: In the Hall of the Mind, several Patients lie in a catatonic state, including Alissa of Norfolk. Alice Liddell will not seek confrontation with intruders but will be friendly and willing to listen to unexpected guests.

Alice's Tactics:

  • Alice will offer tea mixed with a powerful sleep drug (Threat - Alice's special tea, OL Hard 7). Those who fail their Defense Check fall into deep sleep for over eight hours. Only severe pain (1 Wound) or magic can awaken them.
  • Alternatively, she will try to take control of Alicia and pass her off as Alissa, claiming Alicia is a second personality Alissa created inside Wonder to escape from herself.
  • Alice will try not to give the real Alissa to the Hunters, but she won't be willing to lose Wonder to keep her.

Negotiation: If cornered, the Director will be ready to make deals, such as freeing Alissa or whatever the Hunters ask. Alice's real worry is that Wonder might be destroyed.

WonderBedlam - The Living Entity

Descriptor: I am a building animated by sheer madness.

Gift - Place of Pure Madness: Wonder itself is a conscious entity that resists structural alterations. When anyone attempts to alter Wonder's structure (breaking walls, circumventing passages, forcing doors), they trigger this Gift.

Mechanics:

  1. Teleportation (roll 1d6):
    • 1-2: Teleported to the Rabbit Hole
    • 3-4: Teleported to the Labyrinth of Doors
    • 5-6: Teleported to the Caterpillars' Greenhouse
  2. Side Effect (roll 1d6, lasts entire Scene):
    • 1-2 Shrunk: Reduced to half normal size, diminished physical abilities
    • 3-4 Enlarged: Doubled in size, awkward and slow movements
    • 5-6 Confused: Cannot concentrate properly, misunderstand words and intentions

Architectural Impossibilities:

  • Windows and doors painted on exterior—only main entrance is real
  • Rooms appear and disappear at random
  • Corridors don't lead to discernible areas
  • Time flows differently between rooms (moments feel like hours)
  • Day changes to night randomly between rooms
  • Cheshire uses narrow conduits throughout to move unseen

Note: Cheshire the lynx is immune to the Place of Pure Madness effect and can move freely through Wonder's structure.

Scenario Events

In this Scenario, there are five Events. As a Storyteller, feel free to create new ones in response to the actions of the Hunters.

When the Hunters visit the third Cure Room without first meeting Alicia: They will encounter Alicia, who is lost. The young woman will be reluctant to reveal her true identity but will give her name and introduce herself as a patient.

On a regular basis, if the Hunters waste time in the Entrance Hall of Wonder: One of the bedlam Patients will walk out of one of the three doors into another. The Patient will mumble disjointed words such as "see beyond" or "go under".

If the Hunters cause too much havoc in a Cure Room: Alice will take control of Cheshire and order the Hunters to leave, or try to surprise them by making it clear that they are being watched.

If the Hunters try to leave the Basement: A desperate Hatta Carter will attack them with the help of his Automata to keep his new guests with him.

If the Hunters corner Alice Liddell: The Director will be ready to make deals, such as freeing Alissa or whatever the Hunters ask. Alice's real worry is that Wonder might be destroyed.

Exclusive Scenario Gifts

These Gifts represent special abilities related to the Wonderbedlam Scenario and can be acquired once the conditions are met. A Hunter can only acquire one Scenario Gift. If they buy a new one during an Interlude, they will have to forgo the previous one.

Friend of the Rabbits

Requirement: To have captured at least one White Rabbit.

You are able to approach the little white rabbits without frightening them. Receive 2 Additional Successes when you face the Threat The White Rabbit. Once picked up, your new little friends will be able to deliver a message for you to any room in Wonder. This Gift allows you to send a White Rabbit even through seemingly closed doors.

Lucid Madness

Requirement: Having suffered the Gift Place of Pure Madness at least once.

You are slowly figuring out how to make the weirdest ideas real. You can spend 1 Soma to try to activate the mad magic of Wonder. State something crazy that you are capable of, such as "I want to walk on walls" or "I want my arm to stretch out of proportion." The Storyteller will ask you for a Position Check against an OL proportional to the madness of your request. If you succeed, you get the Descriptor you declared for the rest of the Scene; if you fail, you have spent your Soma in vain. The effect of this Gift also ends when someone makes clear that what you are doing is utter madness.

The Forgotten Queen (Special Gift for Regina the Thief of Hearts)

Requirement: Visit a Cure Room.

Confusing flashbacks and painful emotions grip your mind. You've been here as a patient before, but maybe you've forgotten why. Are you sure you want to remember? Once per Scene, you can try to remember something of your experience in Wonder. Choose a place or a subject from the bedlam, and the Storyteller will give you useful information about a Descriptor, a Gift, or their Agenda. Remembering is painful. For the rest of the Scene, when you use one of your Gifts, roll a die: on a 5 or higher you lose 1 Soma and you are no longer so sure that you are the Queen.

FIRST SCENE – Arrival in Wonder

Read aloud to the Hunters:

The structure of Wonder stands out immediately from the houses around it. A modern building, built specifically as a bedlam for the criminally insane, its walls have been painted white with abstract decorations reminiscent of playing cards. The only access seems to be through the main entrance, which is wide open. After entering, you find yourself in a large entrance hall, where to the north a gigantic mirror covers the entire wall. To the south and east, three doors lead into Wonder. There is one the same size as a normal door, one that is twice as large, and one half as large. In the center of the hall stands a tree that acts as a fountain, with water pouring into a central tub. Inside the tub swim several fish of different species, including saltwater fish, swimming as if nothing is wrong. Surprisingly, on the floor, a door is painted, and around it, the following words can be read: "In this place, madness becomes reason."

Place: The Entrance Hall of Wonder.

Time: The Hunters arrive in the morning, but it is clear that time behaves differently here.

Information: After entering, the Hunters will have to wait about 30 minutes. At that point, a white rabbit with a small message holder around its neck will enter from one of the three doors and, with a leap, deliver a message to one of the Hunters, only to flee from the door to the east. The message reads, "You are not welcome at Wonder. The Director."

Wandering around Wonder, the Hunters will soon notice that on the outside of the building, several windows and many doors are painted on the walls, though none of them actually exist. The only real access to the bedlam is through the main entrance. A Hunter who can perceive magic will notice that a powerful arcane force runs throughout the whole building.

End of Scene: For the Hunters, it will be clear that the only way forward is through one of the three doors to the Cure Rooms.

SECOND SCENE – The First Room

Read aloud to the Hunters:

You walk through a long corridor that leads you in front of a door exactly the same as the one you just went through. As in the Entrance Hall, time seems to flow differently in this place. You have the impression that the few steps necessary to cross between one door and the other takes hours rather than moments. You open the door and what is shown to you...

Note: As the Storyteller, continue the description based on the room that was chosen by the Hunters.

Place: One of the Cure Rooms (Rabbit Hole, Labyrinth of Doors, or Caterpillars' Greenhouse).

Time: It is impossible to establish the exact time and, from one room to another, day changes to night in a totally random way.

Information: The Hunters encounter the result 1-2 of the relevant room (see Locations page). Once the encounter is resolved they will notice a White Rabbit and, by capturing it, they will get the first part of the hint to enter the Basement.

End of Scene: It will be clear that each Cure Room holds a clue to progress through Wonder. The Hunters must move to another room. Go to THIRD SCENE – The Second Room, the Encounter with Alicia.

THIRD SCENE – The Second Room, the Encounter with Alicia

Read aloud to the Hunters:

You walk through a long corridor that leads you in front of a door exactly the same as the one you just went through. As in the Entrance Hall, time seems to flow differently in this place and you have the impression that the few steps necessary to cross between one door and the other takes hours rather than moments. You open the door and immediately you are alerted by the cry of a girl.

Note: As the Storyteller, continue the description based on the room that was chosen by the Hunters and highlight the presence of Alicia engaged in an encounter, as a result of 3-4 of the relevant room.

Place: One of the Cure Rooms.

Time: It is impossible to establish the exact time and, from one room to another, day changes to night in a totally random way.

Information: The Hunters are aware that without their intervention the young woman will succumb.

  • Once safe, Alicia will introduce herself, saying that she is in Wonder to save a friend from this place of madness. Alicia has two clues with her, one from the White Rabbit in this room and one from the one the Hunters have not yet visited.
  • During the discussion with Alicia, Alice will attack the girl by surprise via Cheshire, and then make the lynx flee through a wall.

End of Scene: It should now be clear to the Hunters how to access the Basement (Alicia can help them if they haven't quite figured out how to do it yet). Go to FOURTH SCENE - The Basement.

FOURTH SCENE – The Basement

Read aloud to the Hunters:

When the door opens, you see a large flight of stairs that descend underground. A few minutes later you find yourself in a large room, richly furnished with colored cushions and drapes. The Basement is hard to see across, so much so that you can't make out the walls. Suddenly, a shrill voice calls for your attention. In front of you, a gentleman with a strange top hat on his head converses amiably with two Automata, one in the form of a large rabbit, the other of a lizard. In a grotesque imitation of teatime, the man with the top hat conducts a one-way conversation, where his words are left unanswered by the cold, unintelligent gazes of the Automata.

Place: The Basement of Wonder, where those who have passed the Cure Rooms can rest while waiting to be picked up.

Time: It is not possible to distinguish between day and night, but the atmosphere and the soft lighting recall the time for evening tea.

Information: Hatta Carter will be delighted to have new guests and will invite the Hunters to join him for tea. By talking to him you can get the following information:

  • Those who pass through the cycle of therapies in the Cure Rooms can access the Basement where they can refresh themselves, dress again, and wait for the transfer to the Hall of the Mind. The Director keeps Hatta tucked away for future expansions.
  • Hatta is a prisoner of the Director. He built Wonder and knows the formula for going beyond the mirror, accessing the part of Wonder that is exclusive to the Director.
  • If the Hunters point out to Hatta that if he knows every secret, he can escape whenever he wishes, the man will nod in amazement, revealing the formula for going beyond the mirror to the Director's sanctum. Once this is done, however, he will return to his madness, complaining once more about his imprisonment.
  • Hatta will say that he doesn't know the Director's real name, but he knows that she is a renowned doctor and that she is not really a bad person. As a scientist, he understands well that indelicate acts must sometimes be done in the name of scientific progress.
  • The tablecloth on the table is actually the blueprint of Wonder drawn by Hatta during construction. It reveals the formula for going beyond the mirror. The mad inventor, however, will strongly object if the Hunters try to take the map, as this would ruin the table's setting for tea.

Confrontation: When the Hunters attempt to leave the Basement, Hatta will beg them to stay with him, insisting until he becomes aggressive and unleashes the Automata against the Hunters.

End of Scene: After Hatta and his Automata are dealt with, the Hunters know the formula to cross the mirror and finally discover who is behind Wonder. Go to FIFTH SCENE - The Director.

FIFTH SCENE – The Director

Read aloud to the Hunters:

In front of the large mirror on the north wall, you say the formula "Beyond the madness, beyond the basement, beyond the mirror," and you see a small door appear. Crossing through it, you find yourself in a bare and aseptic environment with walls, furniture, and doors, all in white. The doors themselves do not have handles and only with a careful inspection is it possible to distinguish them from the surrounding walls. Finally, you arrive in a huge room, furnished solely with an endless series of beds on which placid patients are sleeping. The distant ticking of a clock breaks an otherwise unnatural silence. Then, a door opens and a distinguished-looking woman with a cold gaze appears in front of you. "Welcome to Wonder. I'm the Director."

Place: The aseptic Hall of the Mind of Wonder.

Time: It is not possible to distinguish between day and night, but the atmosphere and the white lights recall a visit to a hospital.

Information: Alice will offer her guests a fine cup of tea, trying to put them to sleep (Threat - Alice's special tea, OL Hard 7). Those who fail fall into deep sleep for eight hours. Alternatively, she will try to take control of Alicia and pass her off as Alissa. The Director will claim that Alicia is a second personality Alissa has created inside Wonder to escape from herself. Alice will try not to give the real Alissa to the Hunters, but she won't be willing to lose Wonder to keep her.

Negotiation: If cornered, Alice will make deals—freeing Alissa or granting other requests. Her primary concern is protecting Wonder from destruction.

Resolution: The fate of Alissa, Alicia, Alice, Hatta, and Wonder itself rests in the Hunters' hands. Will they destroy the bedlam, reform it, or find another solution?

flags.inspiration: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland by Lewis Carroll
